decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.16°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to maximize the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader, which is currently at its upper bound but can be increased within the allowed 2.0x step ratio to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature. This change respects the 2.0x single-step ratio constraint.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity values assume ideal material behavior; real-world materials might not reach 1000 W/(m*K).
